summary: ByteDance officially launched Seed 2.0 and previewed Dola-Seed-2.0 on Arena, marking a notable new frontier-model push from one of China’s largest consumer internet platforms. OpenAI also launched its Safety Fellowship to fund independent alignment talent development, while a new robotics system, GEN-1, reported 99% task reliability on manipulation work such as box folding and vacuum repair. Separately, Zero Shot, a fund tied to OpenAI alumni, is reportedly targeting $100 million, signaling continued capital formation around the OpenAI ecosystem.

The company also released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, indicating it is putting the new model family into public comparative evaluation environments.
The same announcement stream included ByteDance Seed’s 2027 foundation-model campus recruiting push, suggesting the company is pairing model release cadence with long-horizon talent expansion.

top_topics.HEADLINE: OpenAI Launches the OpenAI Safety Fellowship
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: OpenAI described the Safety Fellowship as a pilot program focused on supporting independent safety and alignment research.
The program is explicitly aimed at developing the next generation of safety talent, which matters for labs, universities, and nonprofits competing for the same researchers.
Because the announcement came directly from OpenAI, it is a verified strategic initiative rather than a speculative hiring signal.

top_topics.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
top_topics.summary: A robotics model called GEN-1 reportedly achieved 99% reliability on tasks such as folding boxes and fixing vacuums. The report says the model can handle disruptions and infer actions beyond its explicit training examples.

items.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
items.summary: New model can respond to disruptions and figure out moves it wasn't trained for.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: A robotics model called GEN-1 reportedly achieved 99% reliability on tasks such as folding boxes and fixing vacuums. The report says the model can handle disruptions and infer actions beyond its explicit training examples.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: If the 99% reliability figure holds up, robotics buyers and investors should pay attention to embodied AI systems that can handle disruptions and out-of-distribution actions.
top_topics.HEADLINE: GEN-1 Robotics Model Reports 99% Reliability on Real-World Manipulation Tasks
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: GEN-1 was reported to achieve 99% reliability on tasks including folding boxes and fixing vacuums, a notably concrete operational metric for robotics performance.
The system was described as able to respond to disruptions and infer moves it was not explicitly trained on, pointing to stronger generalization than narrow scripted automation.
The artifact here is a named robotics model with a quantified reliability claim, which is more decision-useful than generic humanoid robotics demos.

top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: A new $100 million AI-focused fund tied to former OpenAI personnel is a meaningful capital-allocation signal for founders, co-investors, and acquirers tracking the next startup wave.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Zero Shot Targets a $100M Debut Fund Backed by OpenAI Alumni Network
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: TechCrunch reported that Zero Shot is aiming to raise $100 million for its first fund, giving the market a concrete number rather than a vague emerging-manager story.
The fund reportedly has deep ties to OpenAI alumni and has already begun writing checks, suggesting deployment is underway before a final close.
This is strategically relevant because ex-OpenAI networks are increasingly becoming a distribution channel for early-stage AI company formation and financing.

topics.title: Google AI Edge Gallery
topics.summary:
Google AI Edge Gallery

Terrible name, really great app: this is Google's official app for running their Gemma 4 models (the E2B and E4B sizes, plus some members of the Gemma 3 family) directly on your iPhone.

It works really well. The E2B model is a 2.54GB download and is both fast and genuinely useful.

The app also provides "ask questions about images" and audio transcription (up to 30s) with the two small Gemma 4 models, and has an interesting "skills" demo which demonstrates tool calling against eight different interactive widgets, each implemented as an HTML page (though sadly the source code is not visible): interactive-map, kitchen-adventure, calculate-has...

topics.title: Eight years of wanting, three months of building with AI
topics.summary:
Eight years of wanting, three months of building with AI

Lalit Maganti provides one of my favorite pieces of long-form writing on agentic engineering I've seen in ages.

They spent eight years thinking about and then three months building syntaqlite, which they describe as "high-fidelity devtools that SQLite deserves".

The goal was to provide fast, robust and comprehensive linting and verifying tools for SQLite, suitable for use in language servers and other development tools - a parser, formatter, and verifier for SQLite queries. I've found myself wanting this kind of thing in the pa...

topics.title: VibeVoice Technical Report
topics.summary: This report presents VibeVoice, a novel model designed to synthesize long-form speech with multiple speakers by employing next-token diffusion, which is a unified method for modeling continuous data by autoregressively generating latent vectors via diffusion. To enable this, we introduce a novel continuous speech tokenizer that, when compared to the popular Encodec model, improves data compression by 80 times while maintaining comparable performance. The tokenizer effectively preserves audio fidelity while significantly boosting computational efficiency for processing long sequences. Thus,...

topics.title: MinerU2.5: A Decoupled Vision-Language Model for Efficient High-Resolution Document Parsing
topics.summary: We introduce MinerU2.5, a 1.2B-parameter document parsing vision-language model that achieves state-of-the-art recognition accuracy while maintaining exceptional computational efficiency. Our approach employs a coarse-to-fine, two-stage parsing strategy that decouples global layout analysis from local content recognition. In the first stage, the model performs efficient layout analysis on downsampled images to identify structural elements, circumventing the computational overhead of processing high-resolution inputs. In the second stage, guided by the global layout, it performs targeted con...

topics.SHORT_COMMENT: We introduce MinerU2.5, a 1.2B-parameter document parsing vision-language model that achieves state-of-the-art recognition accuracy while maintaining exceptional computational efficiency. Our approach employs a coarse-to-fine, two-stage parsing strategy that decouples global layout analysis from local content recognition. In the first stage, the model performs efficient layout analysis on downsampled images to identify structural elements, circumventing the computational overhead of processing high-resolution inputs. In the second stage, guided by the global layout, it performs targeted con...

topics.HEADLINE: MinerU2.5: A Decoupled Vision-Language Model for Efficient High-Resolution Document Parsing

topics.title: PaddleOCR-VL: Boosting Multilingual Document Parsing via a 0.9B Ultra-Compact Vision-Language Model
topics.summary: In this report, we propose PaddleOCR-VL, a SOTA and resource-efficient model tailored for document parsing. Its core component is PaddleOCR-VL-0.9B, a compact yet powerful vision-language model (VLM) that integrates a NaViT-style dynamic resolution visual encoder with the ERNIE-4.5-0.3B language model to enable accurate element recognition. This innovative model efficiently supports 109 languages and excels in recognizing complex elements (e.g., text, tables, formulas, and charts), while maintaining minimal resource consumption. Through comprehensive evaluations on widely used public benchm...

topics.title: Generative World Renderer
topics.summary: Scaling generative inverse and forward rendering to real-world scenarios is bottlenecked by the limited realism and temporal coherence of existing synthetic datasets. To bridge this persistent domain gap, we introduce a large-scale, dynamic dataset curated from visually complex AAA games. Using a novel dual-screen stitched capture method, we extracted 4M continuous frames (720p/30 FPS) of synchronized RGB and five G-buffer channels across diverse scenes, visual effects, and environments, including adverse weather and motion-blur variants. This dataset uniquely advances bidirectional renderi...

topics.title: Steerable but Not Decodable: Function Vectors Operate Beyond the Logit Lens
topics.summary: Function vectors (FVs) -- mean-difference directions extracted from in-context learning demonstrations -- can steer large language model behavior when added to the residual stream. We hypothesized that FV steering failures reflect an absence of task-relevant information: the logit lens would fail alongside steering. We were wrong. In the most comprehensive cross-template FV transfer study to date - 4,032 pairs across 12 tasks, 6 models from 3 families (Llama-3.1-8B, Gemma-2-9B, Mistral-7B-v0.3; base and instruction-tuned), 8 templates per task - we find the opposite dissociation: FV steerin...

metadata.comment: Shows function vectors can steer behavior even when the correct answer is not decodable by the logit lens, revealing a computation-vs-decoding dissociation.

topics.title: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
topics.summary: AWS published a technical tutorial on customizing a model for agentic tool calling with serverless workflows in Amazon SageMaker AI. The post describes fine-tuning Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct using RLVR, including dataset preparation, reward design, and implementation details.

items.title: From isolated alerts to contextual intelligence: Agentic maritime anomaly analysis with generative AI
items.summary: This blog post demonstrates how Windward helps enhance and accelerate alert investigation processes by combining geospatial intelligence with generative AI, enabling analysts to focus on decision-making rather than data collection.

items.title: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
items.summary: In this post, we walk through how we fine-tuned Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct for tool calling using RLVR. We cover dataset preparation across three distinct agent behaviors, reward function design with tiered scoring, training configuration and results interpretation, evaluation on held-out data with unseen tools, and deployment.

topics.SHORT_COMMENT: AWS published a technical tutorial on customizing a model for agentic tool calling with serverless workflows in Amazon SageMaker AI. The post describes fine-tuning Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct using RLVR, including dataset preparation, reward design, and implementation details.

paper_spotlight.label: New Frontier Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that appear to open a genuinely new direction, paradigm, or field.

items.title: Rashomon Memory: Towards Argumentation-Driven Retrieval for Multi-Perspective Agent Memory
items.summary: The paper opens a novel direction for agent memory by treating disagreement between memories as first-class structure rather than noise to collapse.
items.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
items.spotlight_comment: The paper opens a novel direction for agent memory by treating disagreement between memories as first-class structure rather than noise to collapse.

items.title: Muon Dynamics as a Spectral Wasserstein Flow
items.summary: Broadly important today and potentially frontier-opening: Develops a theory of spectral gradient normalization by casting Muon and related update rules as Spectral Wasserstein gradient flows.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today and potentially frontier-opening: Develops a theory of spectral gradient normalization by casting Muon and related update rules as Spectral Wasserstein gradient flows.

items.title: Geometric Limits of Knowledge Distillation: A Minimum-Width Theorem via Superposition Theory
items.summary: It proposes a potentially important new bridge between SAE-measured feature geometry and practical distillation limits, which could open a new line of compression theory.
items.spotlight_comment: It proposes a potentially important new bridge between SAE-measured feature geometry and practical distillation limits, which could open a new line of compression theory.

items.title: Robust Learning with Optimal Error

items.title: VibeVoice Technical Report
items.summary: This report presents VibeVoice, a novel model designed to synthesize long-form speech with multiple speakers by employing next-token diffusion, which is a unified method for modeling continuous data by autoregressively generating latent vectors via diffusion. To enable this, we introduce a novel continuous speech tokenizer that, when compared to the popular Encodec model, improves data compression by 80 times while maintaining comparable performance. The tokenizer effectively preserves audio fidelity while significantly boosting computational efficiency for processing long sequences. Thus,...

items.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
items.summary: High throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires batching sufficiently many requests at a time. However, existing systems struggle because the key-value cache (KV cache) memory for each request is huge and grows and shrinks dynamically. When managed inefficiently, this memory can be significantly wasted by fragmentation and redundant duplication, limiting the batch size. To address this problem, we propose PagedAttention, an attention algorithm inspired by the classical virtual memory and paging techniques in operating systems. On top of it, we build vLLM, an LLM serving sys...

items.title: LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation
items.summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems enhance large language models (LLMs) by integrating external knowledge sources, enabling more accurate and contextually relevant responses tailored to user needs. However, existing RAG systems have significant limitations, including reliance on flat data representations and inadequate contextual awareness, which can lead to fragmented answers that fail to capture complex inter-dependencies. To address these challenges, we propose LightRAG, which incorporates graph structures into text indexing and retrieval processes. This innovative framework em...
